templatePlaceholders.THESIS: 不是普通的想法，而是某個意義突然顯現，讓你用新的方式看懂一件事。
templatePlaceholders.LEARNING_POSITION: 把 epiphany 記成「sudden appearance of meaning」：靈光一現不是隨便想到，而是理解突然浮出水面。
templatePlaceholders.CORE_IDEA: An epiphany is a sudden realization or insight, often one that makes a situation feel newly clear.
templatePlaceholders.CONCEPT_FOCUS: 意義、答案或模式突然顯露，改變你對問題的理解。
templatePlaceholders.TONE_REGISTER: 文學、日常、反思與創意語境常見；比 idea 更有瞬間開悟感。
templatePlaceholders.USE_WARNING: 不要把 epiphany 用在每個小想法；它暗示理解有明顯轉折，像燈突然亮起。
templatePlaceholders.SHORT_DEFINITION: A sudden and important realization or insight that reveals the meaning of something.
templatePlaceholders.CONFUSION_NOTE: idea 可以很普通；epiphany 是突然看懂、視角改變的時刻。
templatePlaceholders.ORIGIN_PARAGRAPH: epiphany comes from Greek roots meaning appearance or manifestation. In Christian tradition, Epiphany names a feast of divine manifestation; modern English also uses it for sudden insight.
templatePlaceholders.ORIGIN_MEMORY: 這裡要分清楚：宗教歷史的 manifestation 是來源背景；你平常說 an epiphany，通常是在說理解突然顯現。
templatePlaceholders.MEMORY_HOOK: An epiphany is when the bug stops being random in your mind.
templatePlaceholders.MEMORY_EXPLANATION: 你看了很多 log 都沒有答案，直到某一行突然把整個模式照亮。那一刻不是多一個資訊，而是整個問題換了形狀。
templatePlaceholders.DAILY_USAGE: She had an epiphany about why she kept avoiding the task.
templatePlaceholders.PROFESSIONAL_USAGE: The team had an epiphany when they realized every failure followed a warm reboot.
templatePlaceholders.DOMAIN_USAGE: In literature, an epiphany often marks the moment a character suddenly understands themselves or the world differently.
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE: Epiphany 常和 have、sudden、moment 搭配，強調理解出現的瞬間。
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE_1: 最自然搭配，表示突然領悟。
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE_2: 強調洞見來得很快、轉折明顯。
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE_3: 常用在敘事或反思，描述理解顯現的瞬間。
templatePlaceholders.NEIGHBOR_SELF: 突然顯現的洞見，改變理解
templatePlaceholders.NEIGHBOR_SELF_USE: 當一個人突然看懂模式、意義或自己真正的問題時
templatePlaceholders.NEIGHBOR_1_USE: 普通想到一個方案，不一定有理解轉折時
templatePlaceholders.MODERN_USE_1: Modern English uses epiphany for personal insight, creative realization, debugging breakthroughs, and literary moments of revelation.
templatePlaceholders.MODERN_USE_2: The word works best when the insight changes the frame, not merely when someone remembers a fact.
templatePlaceholders.DICTIONARY_SOURCE_NOTE: Dictionary definitions support both the religious manifestation sense and the modern sudden-insight sense.
templatePlaceholders.ETYMOLOGY_SOURCE_NOTE: Etymology notes connect the word to Greek roots for appearance or manifestation.
templatePlaceholders.MODERN_SOURCE_NOTE: Modern examples support use for sudden realizations and moments of insight.
templatePlaceholders.CHECK_MEANING: 我能說出 epiphany 和 idea 的差異。
templatePlaceholders.CHECK_ORIGIN: 我記得它的來源有 manifestation / appearance 的畫面。
templatePlaceholders.CHECK_SENTENCE: 我能用它造一句學習、debug 或人生反思情境的英文句子。
templatePlaceholders.PRACTICE_SENTENCE: The epiphany came when we noticed that every reset happened after the same interrupt.

indexEntry.thesis: 不是普通的想法，而是某個意義突然顯現，讓你用新的方式看懂一件事。

checks.label: 說出 epiphany 和 idea 的差異

用它造一句學習、debug 或人生反思情境的英文句子
